title: Hide boardname in Dashboard Timeline widget
excerpt:

posts.author: Jona67 (Jonathan Wu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dashboard timeline widget by default shows the board name of an item/event.
(in the format of “[Item name] | [Board name]”)

This is useful if I use one combined dashboard view to manage multiple board sources.
But in cases where I’m only connecting to one board source, having the board name repeated for every item is pretty redundant and space consuming.

Is there anyway to not show the board source of the items?

–example–
Say I have Item 1~10 from board “ABC”.
The timeline would show: Item 1 | ABC, Item 2 | ABC, Item 3 | ABC…etc.
and obviously repeating “ABC” is unnecessary.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Jona67, thanks for posting about this!

Unfortunately there’s no way to not show the board source of the items—that will pull in automatically.

posts.author: JulieMars (Julie Mars)
posts.cookedHtml: are there any plans on changing that in future please? i have the same issue when i’m trying to build a clean view.

posts.author: IzzyLu (Isabell Lu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Since I need this functionality as well, I created a feature request.
